title: trigger formatToDisplay
text: trigger formatToDisplay System trigger that temporarily converts the format of data in a field from an internal format into an external format that is displayed or printed. Declaration: trigger formatToDisplay Applies to: Field Activation: Activated when the form is initially displayed, each time an occurrence becomes active, after the loseFocus trigger, and after the formatFromDisplay trigger to restore the value of $format . Default behavior: None Behavior upon completion: None Description The formatToDisplay trigger is used to convert data from the internal format used by Uniface to an external (displayed or printed) format. It is activated whenever Uniface needs to display the field. The formatToDisplay and formatFromDisplay triggers are especially useful with custom widgets. Any ValRep mapping for widgets happens after formatToDisplay and before formatFromDisplay. Note: The formatFromDisplay trigger should undo the formatting done here. When the trigger is activated, the function $format contains the data. This data has been formatted according to the display (DIS) property of the field's layout definition. You may use ProcScript or 3GL code to modify the data in $format before it is displayed. When the trigger completes, $format should contain the data formatted for display. CAUTION: The formatToDisplay trigger should be used only for formatting of the associated field. Do not use this trigger for formatting other fields or for other field-related processing. While a component is being populated, a field's formatToDisplay trigger may be activated in several occurrences. During this activity, the occurrence that is current in the form remains unchanged. This means that if you reference another field from the formatToDisplay trigger, you always reference that field in the current occurrence, which is not necessarily the same occurrence as the field whose formatToDisplay trigger is being activated. Related Links trigger formatFromDisplay System trigger that temporarily converts the format of data in a field from an external format into an internal format, and makes that reformatted data available for processing purposes.
